mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to zero during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative risk sentiment and downward pressure on S&P 500 prices over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp monthly rise threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in > 55% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 during confirmed expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 235 signal persistent inflationary pressures that force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 235 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending capacity and supporting positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when PAYEMS increases while UNRATE declines simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show zero or negative correlation with subsequent 20-day equity returns across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and manufacturing slowdown, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a > 1.0% monthly decline in INDPRO.
